source: Oregon Child Support Guidelines Scale (Table 1), OAR 137-050-0725, https://justice.oregon.gov/child-support/pdf/guidelines_scale.pdf (live URL broken from this environment -- fetched via Wayback Machine's 2025-06-28 snapshot of the official PDF; cross-checked the $30,000 cap and 10-children coverage against the CURRENTLY live OAR 137-050-0725 rule text, confirming this is still the legally operative schedule, not a superseded one -- a newer $50,000-cap/6-children scale is under active Guidelines Advisory Committee review as of 2026 but not yet adopted into the rule)
note: All 581 real rows transcribed directly from the full official scale ($1,000-$30,000 combined monthly gross adjusted income, $50 increments, 10 children columns), extracted via pdftotext -layout, not OCR. OAR 137-050-0725(5) directs using the LOWER listed amount for income falling between two amounts; since the table is printed as contiguous ranges already, a ceiling lookup on each range's upper bound (stepBrackets) reproduces this correctly. Below $1,000/mo the schedule is flat at $50 (self-support-reserve floor).
